name: Extract from receipts with Google Drive with instant alerts

parameters.text: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 12 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Export All Rows (Google Sheets)

### Export All Rows (Google Sheets)

Reads every row from 'Document Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Next: Serialize to CSV

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Serialize to CSV

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Upload Backup to Google Drive

### Upload Backup to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Next: Backup Size Sane?

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Upload Backup to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Backup Size Sane?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($binary.data?.fileSize ?? 1) > 0 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Stamp Processing Time · ②→ Alarm — Empty Backup

### Alarm — Empty Backup

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Sort Documents Newest First

### Sort Documents Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

# Extract from receipts with Google Drive with instant alerts

**Pattern:** File Processing · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Extract from receipts with Google Drive with instant alerts. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual summarization is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Export All Rows (Google Sheets) → Serialize to CSV → Upload Backup to Google Drive → Backup Size Sane? → Stamp Processing Time → Sort Documents Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to file processing without manual effort
- Anyone automating summarization in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The empty-backup check exists because a backup that silently 'succeeds' with zero rows is worse than an obvious failure — it gives false confidence right up until the day you actually need to restore. Compression runs before upload, not after, cutting both transfer time and storage footprint in the same step.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Point the export at an empty table on purpose and confirm the alarm fires.
2. Turn off compression and compare the resulting backup file size.
3. Add a retention step that deletes backups older than N days from the destination.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your summarization process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
